rationale: Identified the large center house with complex tan/brown hip roof as the subject property. Bounding box estimated at approximately 365 px (E–W) × 455 px (N–S) = 166,075 sq px. Due to the highly stepped, multi-section shape with numerous bump-outs and recesses, a 58% fill factor was applied yielding ~96,323 sq px × 0.038093 = ~3,669 sq ft footprint. No gable ends are visible, confirming a pure hip roof with zero rake edges. Ridge segments were counted across each sub-roof peak (~8 segments). Hip edges were tallied at roughly 4 per sub-section across ~6–7 major intersecting sections. Valley lines were identified at each inward roof intersection (~9 total). Eave perimeter was traced around the entire stepped ground-level outline. Confidence is moderate-low (0.40) due to the extreme roof complexity, oblique viewing angle, and overlapping shadows making precise edge tracing difficult.
